name: New Mexico RLD Construction Industries License Verification

agency.name: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department

updateFrequency: unknown; verify against the official Construction Industries verification path before relying on freshness assumptions
licenseTypesIncluded: New Mexico Construction Industries Division license records exposed by the official verification path
General construction, electrical, mechanical/plumbing, LP gas, and specialty classifications identified by CID bureau pages and 14.6.6 NMAC
knownExclusions: The RLD verification page routes Construction Industries Division verification to an external PSI search path.
Local permits, business registrations, and non-RLD authorizations are outside this source entry.
RLD boards and commissions license lookup is separate from the Construction Industries Division path and may need separate entries.
Classification and qualifying-party details must be preserved before normalization.
Solar coverage appears through electrical, mechanical/plumbing, and specialty scopes; do not flatten photovoltaic and solar-thermal work into one undifferentiated category.
rateLimitNotes: No adapter is implemented. Do not automate external lookup paths unless terms and technical controls are reviewed.
publicRecordsNotes: Official lookup source metadata only. Absence from this source is not proof that a license or permit does not exist elsewhere.
officialBulkDownloadNotes: No official bulk download has been confirmed in this phase.
researchNotes: Registry-only source for New Mexico Construction Industries verification. CID pages and 14.6.6 NMAC identify statewide general building, residential, commercial, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, mechanical, roofing, pool/spa, sheet-metal, underground-utility, asbestos-removal, solar-relevant, and home-improvement-relevant classifications.
maintainerNotes: Keep the RLD-versus-PSI lookup split visible in future docs and verification behavior. Preserve CID classification and qualifying-party context if adapter work begins.

researchEvidence.note: Official public source, access shape, and current automation constraints reviewed.
Official CID bureau page states construction-related contracting in New Mexico must be licensed and lists general construction, electrical, LP gas, and mechanical plumbing bureaus.
Official 14.6.6 NMAC classification rule defines CID general construction, electrical, mechanical/plumbing, and specialty classification scopes.
Official NMED asbestos page states contractors who remove asbestos materials must be licensed by the New Mexico Construction Industries Division.

blocker.summary: No stable, documented machine-readable official interface is currently suitable for an offline-tested adapter.
